heading: Generally applicable remedies and limitations

children.heading: Attorney’s fees
children.label: a Attorney’s fees
children.text: If a covered employee, with respect to any claim under this chapter, or a qualified person with a disability, with respect to any claim under , is a prevailing party in any proceeding under section 1405, 1406, 1407, or 1408 of this title, the hearing officer, Board, or court, as the case may be, may award attorney’s fees, expert fees, and any other costs as would be appropriate if awarded under .section 1331 of this titlesection 2000e–5(k) of title 42

children.heading: Interest
children.label: b Interest
children.text: In any proceeding under section 1405, 1406, 1407, or 1408 of this title, the same interest to compensate for delay in payment shall be made available as would be appropriate if awarded under .section 2000e–16(d) of title 42

children.heading: Civil penalties and punitive damages
children.label: c Civil penalties and punitive damages
children.text: No civil penalty or punitive damages may be awarded with respect to any claim under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no person may commence an administrative or judicial proceeding to seek a remedy for the rights and protections afforded by this chapter except as provided in this chapter.

children.heading: Veterans
children.label: 2 Veterans
children.text: A covered employee under may also utilize any provisions of chapter 43 of title 38 that are applicable to that employee.section 1316 of this title

children.heading: Definitions and exemptions
children.label: 1 Definitions and exemptions
children.text: Except where inconsistent with definitions and exemptions provided in this chapter, the definitions and exemptions in the laws made applicable by this chapter shall apply under this chapter.

children.heading: Size limitations
children.label: 2 Size limitations
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), provisions in the laws made applicable under this chapter (other than the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act [ et seq.]) determining coverage based on size, whether expressed in terms of numbers of employees, amount of business transacted, or other measure, shall not apply in determining coverage under this chapter.29 U.S.C. 2101

children.heading: Executive branch enforcement
children.label: 3 Executive branch enforcement
children.text: This chapter shall not be construed to authorize enforcement by the executive branch of this chapter.
